key_insight: The paper builds a unified convergence framework for decentralized stochastic subgradient methods with compressed communication on nonsmooth, nonconvex objectives.
engineering_relevance: It offers practical distributed optimization tools for bandwidth-limited systems where communication efficiency must be balanced against solution accuracy.
method: Unifies decentralized stochastic subgradient-type algorithms under one analysis for unbiased and contractive compression.
Uses consensus-error and averaged-iterate dynamics linked to continuous-time differential inclusions to prove global convergence.
Develops compression-based variants with sign-based regularization and gradient-tracking momentum.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports preliminary numerical experiments that empirically validate the theory and illustrate the communication-accuracy trade-off.
watch_item: Performance and convergence speed may depend strongly on the compression scheme, error compensation, and the nonsmoothness of the objective in practice.
paper_title: Decentralized Stochastic Subgradient-type Methods with Communication Compression for Nonsmooth Nonconvex Optimization
